data.description: Many countries across Europe are turning to hydropower and energy storage to bolster their national grids. In Greece, the focus is on upgrading existing systems and building storage facilities to take advantage of wind and solar. CGTN’s correspondent Evangelo Sipsas reports.

data.title: GA completes the world's largest and most powerful pulsed superconducting magnet for fusion energy at nearly 60ft tall.
data.description: In San Diego, the team at General Atomics (GA) is thrilled to announce a major accomplishment as they have finished constructing the Central Solenoid Modules, a key component of the largest and most potent pulsed superconducting magnet ever created. Standing at almost 60 feet in height, this Central Solenoid is set to drive fusion reactions at ITER, a global fusion research facility being built in southern France. This milestone highlights the innovation of California's engineering and science sector.

data.title: Snowflake (NYSE: SNOW) beats Q2 expectations, raises full-year revenue forecast. CEO claims it's the best AI data platform. Stock surges.
data.description: Snowflake, Inc., a prominent player in the cloud data warehouse industry listed as NYSE: SNOW, surpassed expectations in the second quarter in terms of top and bottom lines. They also raised their full-year product revenue forecast. The company's stock, SNOW, is experiencing significant growth. Snowflake CEO Sridhar Ramaswamy stated confidently during the latest earnings call that Snowflake is the top AI data platform available, a sentiment echoed by many existing and potential customers. Ramaswamy's assertion was supported by strong developments. Check out the real-time price fluctuations. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
The DoE issues an emergency order to secure grid reliability, directing PJM to keep Eddystone power units running. The order is to continue until November 26th.

data.title: CFTC permits US citizens to trade on Binance and other foreign crypto exchanges, opening doors for overseas firms.
data.description: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) has issued new guidelines that pertain to cryptocurrency exchanges operating within the United States. This guidance opens up opportunities for foreign companies to enter the US market legally. This development is significant as it potentially allows a wide range of international firms to establish a presence in the country's burgeoning crypto industry.
WH Press Sec. Leavitt ends press briefing.

data.title: U.S. equities at record highs amidst warning signs - 446 bankruptcies in 2025, low market breadth, stretched valuations. S&P 500-to-Gold ratio signals generational alarm.
data.description: U.S. equities are reaching new highs despite concerning indicators. Bankruptcy filings have increased, with 446 large companies collapsing in 2025. Market breadth levels are at 2008 lows, and valuations are stretched with Robert Shiller's CAPE ratio near dot-com peaks. Warren Buffett's market cap-to-GDP gauge is also signaling a warning. The exuberant optimism is causing alarm. Professor Steve Hanke warns against trying to time bubbles, as pulling out and getting back in often yields poor results. The S&P 500-to-Gold ratio is now flashing red. Danish economist Henrik Zeberg sees this as a once-in-a-generation signal. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
